title: Cameron Hayman
text:
Welcome
Welcome to my quantitative research and data science portfolio.
Here you’ll find a deep dive into my work across:

Systematic trading

Statistical arbitrage

Predictive modeling

Real-time execution systems


Explore

Advanced trading systems using latency arbitrage

Alternative data alpha extraction pipelines using NLP

Monte Carlo & GARCH-based forecasting engines

Full-stack quant research dashboards (Prometheus, Grafana, Shiny)

All systems have been built for real-world execution, blending research rigor with production readiness.

title: About Me
text:
I’m a Statistical Arbitrage Quantitative Researcher focused on transforming advanced statistical and machine learning research into high-performance, production-grade trading strategies.
My expertise spans the entire quant lifecycle:

Signal generation & factor modeling

Strategy backtesting (Python, R, SQL)

Microsecond-level latency arbitrage

Live execution pipelines using Prometheus, Docker, and Grafana





Trading Strategies: Mean reversion, cross-sectional momentum, pricing inefficiencies

Alt Data & NLP: 10-Q parsing, earnings call tone analysis, SEC filings

ML Modeling: LSTMs, GARCH, Monte Carlo, ensemble methods

Infrastructure: Docker, Postgres, GitHub Actions, ClickHouse, Prometheus

My mission is to bridge quant theory with infrastructure engineering to deploy strategies that work in the real world.

title: About Me
text:

title: Projects
text:
Project Portfolio



Systematic Stat Arb Trade Desk: Full-stack execution system deploying real-time mean reversion and momentum strategies using Python, R, and SQL.





Geo-Spatial Latency Arbitrage: Optimizes cross-market spreads with real-time rerouting using Prometheus, PowerShell, and Grafana.





Alt Data NLP Pipeline: Extracts alpha from 10-Qs, earnings calls, and filings using transformer models and integrates them into multifactor engines.





Monte Carlo + GARCH Forecasting: Equity price prediction engine combining time-series simulation and stochastic volatility models (R, ggplot2).
